title: SENTINEL APEX — Exploit for CVE-2017-0144 exploit
id: 9f0ee404-fc9c-53c7-838b-c3af96ee3832
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2017-0144 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: KEV_EXPLOIT
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-0144
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/20
modified: 2026/07/20
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2017-0144 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-20T01:55:15Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2017-0144", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2017-0144 Exploitation Attempt - KEV_EXPLOIT"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:3434238; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-20, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Prompty: Arbitrary code execution via JavaScript frontmatter in TypeScript loader
items.description:
## Summary
The TypeScript Prompty loader used `gray-matter` without overriding executable frontmatter engines. `gray-matter` supports JavaScript frontmatter blocks such as `---js` and evaluates them while parsing. An attacker-controlled `.prompty` file could therefore execute arbitrary JavaScript during prompt loading.

## Affected package
- npm `@prompty/core` v2 prerelease line: `>= 2.0.0-alpha.1 < 2.0.0-beta.3`
- Fixed in `@prompty/core@2.0.0-beta.3`

The legacy v1 JavaScript runtime had a historical hardening change for this issue. During the v2 TypeScript runtime rebuild, the loader again called `gray-matter` directly and the vulnerable behavior was present in the v2 prerelease packages until `2.0.0-beta.3`.

## Impact
Applications that load untrusted `.prompty` files, user-provided prompt paths, or prompt bundles from less-trusted locations could execute arbitrary JavaScript in the host Node.js process during frontmatter parsing.

## Remediation
Upgrade `@prompty/core` to `2.0.0-
items.confidence_rationale: Single source with minimal corroborating evidence

items.title: CVE-2026-47871: VMware Avi Load Balancer contains a directory traversal vulnerability. Flaws in file path validation allow malicious, au
items.description:
VMware Avi Load Balancer contains a directory traversal vulnerability. Flaws in file path validation allow malicious, authenticated network users to perform directory traversal attacks.

Affected versions:
32.1.1 (fixed in 32.1.2)
31.1.1 through 31.2.2 (fixed in 31.2.2-2p3)
30.1.1 through 30.2.6 (fixed in 30.2.7)
22.1.1 through 22.1.7 (fixed in 30.2.7)

items.title: Critical NGINX Vulnerability Can Crash Workers and May Allow Remote Code Execution
items.description:
F5 has shipped fixes for a critical nginx flaw that lets a remote, unauthenticated attacker trigger a heap buffer overflow in the worker process with crafted HTTP requests. CVE-2026-42533 was patched on July 15 in nginx 1.30.4 (stable) and 1.31.3 (mainline), and in NGINX Plus 37.0.3.1; anyone on an earlier build should upgrade.

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Critical NGINX Vulnerability Can Crash Workers and May Allow Remote Code Executi
id: 05c6a1b5-3056-576b-9266-cb9759a501f2
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-42533 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-42533
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/20
modified: 2026/07/20
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-42533 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-20T01:55:15Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-42533", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-42533 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:7266450; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-20, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.recommended_sla_action: URGENT PATCH — Critical severity, deploy fix within 72 hours
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: CVE-2026-16097: A vulnerability was found in Shibby Tomato 1.28. This vulnerability affects the function sub_42537C of the component Sch
items.description: A vulnerability was found in Shibby Tomato 1.28. This vulnerability affects the function sub_42537C of the component Scheduler Name Handler. The manipulation of the argument a1 results in stack-based buffer overflow. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. This project is superseded by FreshTomato.

// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-16097 — Anomalous Activity Detection

| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
